figures.caption: Fig. 1. Image-based target localization without dense 3D reconstruction. Using only posed RGB-D keyframes as a lightweight visual memory, our method directly infers the 3D target location and enables a robot to navigate to the queried object without building a global scene map.

Fig. 4. Qualitative comparison with HOV-SG [17]. Our method retrieves the correct view with segmentation mask and produces a fused 3D point cloud, capturing fine-grained details that HOV-SG overlooks.
TABLE IV MEMORY-ACCURACY TRADE-OFF AND PIPELINE EFFICIENCY.

Fig. 3. Qualitative retrieval examples. Top-1 retrieved images for naturallanguage queries across small indoor (HM3D [51], MP3D [52]), large indoor (LaMAR-CAB [59]), and outdoor (LaMAR-LIN [59]) scenes.

Fig. 2. Method overview. Given a query and posed RGB-D keyframes: (1) Retrieval: SigLIP2 embeddings indexed with FAISS retrieve top-K candidates. (2) VLM Re-Rank: a VLM filters false positives (red) and promotes true matches (green). (3) 3D Localization: SAM 3 segments the target; masked depth is backprojected, predictions are grouped into object instances, and per-instance multi-view fusion produces a 3D goal estimate. No global 3D map is built. (4) Navigation: a PointNav policy navigates directly to the goal. (Scene point cloud shown for visualization only.)

Fig. 5. Real-world robot navigation. Spot navigates to queried objects in an iPad-scanned indoor scene. (a) Third-person view of the robot at the goal. (b) Top-1 retrieved image with segmentation mask (green). (c) Spot’s onboard camera view upon arrival.
Fig. 6. VLM re-ranking ablation. SigLIP2 retrieves semantically similar but incorrect views (red); VLM re-ranking corrects all three queries (green).
TABLE I GOAT-CORE LOCALIZATION. SR@5 (%) ON GOAT-CORE [29]. “–” INDICATES THE METHOD DOES NOT SUPPORT THAT QUERY TYPE.
TABLE III TEXT-TO-IMAGE RETRIEVAL. AR@1 AND AR@5 ON SUN RGB-D [58].
TABLE II OBJECT GOAL NAVIGATION. SR AND SPL (%) ON (A) HM3D [51] & MP3D [52], AND (B) HM3D-OVON [53].
